rationale: Naik and Raman (2003) is cited repeatedly in the theory setup, not just in a background list. The paper relies on it to justify optimal control as the key framework for simultaneous budget and media allocation and to motivate inclusion of media interaction terms in the advertising force function. Although the citing model is developed mainly through its own micro-level reformulation (and also draws on Naik, Raman, and Winer 2005), the 2003 study clearly serves as an important conceptual and methodological base.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Naik and Raman (2003) as a core prior for optimal-control-based multimedia advertising allocation at the macro level. It then positions its own contribution as a generalization from that macro tradition to individual-level microtargeting and references the 2003 paper's interaction-force idea when discussing possible media synergy extensions.
